one_line_takeaway: Two co-trained LLM paraphrasers plus a classifier-as-reward-model (PPO) embed a robust, high-fidelity, variable-length multi-bit watermark that beats prior lexical/LLM-output watermarks on word-substitution robustness and stealth, but — like all semantics-preserving watermarks — remains vulnerable to aggressive re-paraphrasing.
key_finding: On C4 with the 1.1B model the method reaches Bit Acc 0.9563, ~5.57 bits per 128-token text (about 1 bit / 23 tokens), detection AUC 0.9981, TPR@FPR=1% of 98.0% and TPR@FPR=0.01% of 78.0%, and the HIGHEST fidelity of all methods (mpnet similarity 0.8739) — beating every baseline on both bit-wise and text-wise accuracy. Five paraphraser repeats push AUC to 0.9999 and bit accuracy above 0.99. It is markedly more robust to word substitution than baselines (Ours-AdvT keeps AUC 0.9936 at 10% substitution and 0.9853 even at 20%). Stealthiness holds: GPT in-context classification of the two sentence classes reaches only 57.0% (near the 50% random baseline). The paper is candid that SENTENCE paraphrasing attacks hurt much more (AUC drops to 0.931 for translation, 0.865 for Llama paraphrase, 0.862 for Pegasus for the benign model), because any semantics-preserving re-paraphrase can in the limit revert text toward its unwatermarked form.
core_contribution: The paper proposes a multi-bit text watermarking scheme that embeds a binary message by PARAPHRASING the input text, rather than by synonym substitution (lexical methods) or by biasing an LLM's token sampling (LLM-output watermarks like KGW/KTH). The encoder is a PAIR of LLM paraphrasers (theta_0, theta_1) fine-tuned to paraphrase the same text in subtly different ways; a sentence-level text segmentor assigns one watermark bit per sentence and switches between the two paraphrasers accordingly, yielding a variable-length code (longer text carries more bits). The decoder is an LM-based binary text classifier that reads each sentence and predicts its bit. The novel training idea (the authors' own contribution, adapting the co-training framework of Xu et al. 2024) is to treat the decoder/classifier as a REWARD MODEL and use PPO to fine-tune the two paraphrasers so their output is more reliably classifiable, while a co-training loop alternately updates decoder (cross-entropy) and encoder (PPO reward = correctly decoded bits + a text-similarity reward). A JS-divergence-maximizing SFT initialization forces the two paraphrasers to differ before RL begins.
threat_model: The watermark is a provenance/tracing mechanism (copyright protection, tracking LLM misinformation, hidden-message communication). The adversary observes watermarked text and tries to (1) REMOVE the watermark via perturbations that preserve meaning, and (2) DETECT/read the hidden signal. Concretely the paper evaluates removal under random word substitution (5%/10%/20% of tokens replaced with uniformly random vocabulary tokens) and under sentence paraphrasing (Spanish round-trip translation, Llama-2-7B paraphrase, Pegasus paraphrase). Stealthiness is modeled as an adversary (human eyes, or GPT with 10+10 in-context examples) trying to classify which of the two paraphraser 'classes' a sentence belongs to. The stated defender goals are Fidelity (semantic similarity preserved), Accuracy (low bit error), Robustness (low bit error after perturbation), and Stealthiness (adversary's guess on the code has HIGH error). The paper also studies an adversarially trained variant (Ours-AdvT) that sees perturbed text (10% word substitution) when training the decoder.
limitations: (1) Sentence-paraphrasing attacks cause a large, explicitly acknowledged drop — the authors note the extreme case where paraphrasing recovers the original unwatermarked text makes detection impossible in principle; adversarial training only partially recovers this (e.g. Translate AUC 0.9709, but LlamaPara bit-acc still ~0.85). (2) Data-driven / domain-dependent: OOD performance is generally strong (>0.99 AUC on HH, and 0.97-0.99 on PKU/Reward/UltraF/FineWeb) but weakest on Pile (AUC 0.9713), attributed to structural/XML-like text; the authors' remedy is simply to add new domains to training. (3) Scaling to Llama-2-7B gives no gain over 1.1B (AUC 0.9973 vs 0.9981), and the strongest Waterfall setting (kappa=4) becomes competitive at 0.9951 on 7B. (4) The watermark's own Impact Statement notes possible misuse for covert/hidden-message communication. (5) Fidelity-vs-detectability is a genuine trade-off controlled by lambda_s/lambda_k (higher lambda_s raises similarity to 0.9728 but drops bit acc to 0.7606).

datasets_used: C4 RealNewsLike (Raffel et al. 2020) — main training and evaluation, 128-token texts
Pegasus paraphraser (used to generate SFT paraphrase data and as a paraphrasing attack)
quantitative_results: Ours (TinyLlama-1.1B, C4): Bit Acc 0.9563, Bit Num 5.57 (~1 bit / 23 tokens), AUC 0.9981, TPR@FPR=1% 98.0%, TPR@FPR=0.01% 78.0%, mpnet similarity 0.8739
With 5 paraphraser repeats: bit accuracy >0.99, detection AUC 0.9999
Word substitution 10%: Ours AUC 0.9871 / Ours-AdvT AUC 0.9936 (bit acc 0.9352); 20%: Ours AUC 0.9469 / Ours-AdvT AUC 0.9853 (bit acc 0.9138)
Sentence paraphrasing (benign Ours): Translate AUC 0.9310 (bitacc 0.8206), LlamaPara AUC 0.8649 (0.7137), PegasusPara AUC 0.8616 (0.7388); Ours-AdvT improves Translate to AUC 0.9709
OOD AUC: HH 0.9991, PKU 0.9959, Reward 0.9962, UltraF 0.9931, FineWeb 0.9880, Pile 0.9713 (weakest)
Stealthiness: GPT in-context classification accuracy 57.0% on 1,000+1,000 sentences (vs 50% random)
Ablation: lambda_s=5.0 -> similarity 0.9728 but bit acc 0.7606; lambda_s=0.2 -> bit acc 0.9722, similarity 0.8283 (fidelity/detectability trade-off)
Llama-2-7B: Ours AUC 0.9973 (bit acc 0.9605); Waterfall kappa=4 competitive at AUC 0.9951
baselines_compared: RemarkLLM (Zhang et al. 2024b), 4-bit (AUC 0.7861) and 8-bit (AUC 0.8023) T5-based fixed-length watermark
KGW (Kirchenbauer et al. 2023), zero-bit (AUC 0.8652) and multi-bit (AUC 0.8327, bit acc 0.6381)
KTH (Kuditipudi et al. 2023), zero-bit (AUC 0.8919) and multi-bit (AUC 0.6775)
Waterfall (Lau et al. 2024), kappa=0.5 (AUC 0.7787) and kappa=1 (AUC 0.9392), plus kappa=2/4 on the 7B model
